output.final_deliverable:
Source: Visit to Wikipedia Main Page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page)

Collected links (3 of 10 requested - only these were present in the visit result):

1) URL: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias
   Link text/title: MISSING (not provided in the visit result)
   Description: MISSING (no short description/snippet was returned for this link in the merged results)
   Source URL: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias

2) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
   Link text/title: MISSING (not provided in the visit result)
   Description: MISSING (no short description/snippet was returned for this link in the merged results)
   Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents

3) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia
   Link text/title: MISSING (not provided in the visit result)
   Description: MISSING (no short description/snippet was returned for this link in the merged results)
   Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia

Notes and missing items:
- The visit action returned links_count: 20 and provided a links_sample containing the three URLs above, but the merged results do not include the corresponding link text/title for these URLs nor any descriptions/snippets.
- The subsequent attempt to retrieve short descriptions via search failed (Search API error: "Unprocessable Entity"), so the 1-2 sentence descriptions for each link are not available in the provided evidence.
- The user requested 10 links; only 3 link URLs were present in the merged results. The remaining 7 link URLs (and their titles and descriptions) are missing from the evidence.

What is needed to complete the task:
- The extracted link text/title for the 3 URLs above (if available in the Main Page HTML) or a re-run of the Main Page visit that returns full link text/title for at least 10 links.
- Short descriptions (1-2 sentences) for each of the 10 links; these could be obtained either from the individual pages or from search snippets. The previous search attempt failed and returned an error.

If you want, I can: (a) re-run the Main Page visit to extract full link text/title for 10 links, then fetch each article page to produce 1-2 sentence descriptions; or (b) attempt the search/snippet approach again. Please tell me which you prefer.
output.action_summary: I visited the Wikipedia Main Page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and the visit returned a sample of three link URLs and a total links_count of 20. The merged results provide only the three URLs (https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia) but do not include link text/titles or 1-2 sentence descriptions. A follow-up search to get descriptions failed with a Search API error (Unprocessable Entity). Seven of the requested 10 links (and all descriptions and link text for the provided links) are missing from the evidence. I can re-run the extraction and fetch pages/snippets if you want me to proceed.

6864c266-2abe-475a-bacf-2f1c827be572.title: Visit Wikipedia Main Page to extract candidate links

details.intent: Retrieve the full HTML/content of Wikipedia's Main Page and extract inline links (URLs and link text) to produce a candidate list for the 10 links requested.
target_facts: All inline links (URL and visible link text) found on the Main Page (up to ~100 to allow choice)
Context/snippet around each link (to help produce a short 1-2 sentence description)
Structure anchors (e.g., Featured article, In the news, On this day) to prefer prominent links
details.extraction_plan: 1) Fetch full page content. 2) Parse for internal Wikipedia links (<a href="/wiki/..." and absolute) and capture exact absolute URL, visible link text, and a short surrounding text/snippet (20-40 words). 3) Tag links by section (e.g., 'From today's featured article', 'In the news') when available. 4) Produce a ranked candidate set of at least 12–15 links (rank by prominence: featured items, top-left navigation, 'In the news', 'On this day', and other obvious article links) so we can pick the best 10. 5) Output the extracted links in a machine-friendly list including the link URL (absolute), link text/title, and the snippet for verification.
measurable_outputs: Array of extracted link objects: {url, link_text, snippet, section}
Selection criteria applied (which section each chosen link came from)
verification_steps: Confirm each extracted link URL is an absolute HTTPS URL (convert /wiki/... to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/...) only using the exact href as found in the page content.
For each extracted link, check the snippet contains the link text or the article title to ensure correct association.
details.reasoning: Directly visiting the Main Page is required to obtain the exact links and the exact href values that downstream steps must use. This removes uncertainty about which links are present and their canonical URLs; it provides the canonical source for subsequent brief descriptions.
details.parent_goal: Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.

5b194c49-950b-4796-8222-aec1ebd389d8.title: Search for short descriptions (snippets) for the selected links

details.query: For each selected Wikipedia article title/URL returned by the Main Page visit, run a targeted site:wikipedia.org search (or use the page's snippet if the search tool returns the page) to retrieve a concise description (1-2 sentences) about what the page is about. Example query template: "site:wikipedia.org "<Article Title>"". Execute one search per selected article and return top snippet and the source URL.
details.intent: Obtain short, verifiable 1-2 sentence descriptions for each of the 10 chosen links by retrieving authoritative snippets or the page summary from Wikipedia search results.
target_facts: For each chosen link: a short snippet (1-2 sentences) summarizing the page content, and the URL of the source snippet
If the search snippet is insufficient, return an indicator (e.g., 'snippet_incomplete') so a follow-up visit can be scheduled
details.extraction_plan: 1) For each of the 10 selected link titles/URLs from the previous node, run a query using the title and/or the exact URL as a search term restricted to site:wikipedia.org. 2) Extract the top snippet (or the page summary) and the returned result URL. 3) Keep the snippet to 1-2 sentences; if snippet is longer, trim to the most informative sentence(s). 4) Mark any items where the snippet does not look like the article lead (these will be flagged for possible follow-up visits).
measurable_outputs: For each of 10 links: {url, link_text, snippet_1_2_sentences, snippet_source_url, snippet_confidence_flag}
Count of items with high-confidence snippets vs flagged items
verification_steps: Prefer snippets where the snippet_source_url matches the extracted link URL (i.e., the snippet comes from the article itself).
If search returns an external source or disambiguation page, mark for follow-up visit of the actual article page.
details.reasoning: Using targeted searches for each chosen link yields concise lead-summary snippets without requiring separate full-page visits for every article, which is faster and sufficient to produce 1-2 sentence descriptions. If any snippet is ambiguous or insufficient, we will detect that via the confidence flag and then schedule a follow-up visit to the specific article.

0c00fea2-4d65-45f1-95ba-c056b1ba6d86.title: Save compiled results (10 links with URL, title, and 1-2 sentence description)

details.intent: After extracting the 10 links and their descriptions, compile the final organized output (array of 10 objects) and save it to memory/store for retrieval.
target_facts: Final compiled list: for each of 10 entries -> {url, link_text/title, brief_description_1_2_sentences, source_of_description}
details.extraction_plan: 1) Merge outputs from the Main Page visit (link URL and link text) with the search snippets (descriptions). 2) Format each entry into a concise record. 3) Store the array under a descriptive memory key (e.g., 'wikipedia_mainpage_top10_links_YYYYMMDD').
measurable_outputs: A saved metadata record: {timestamp, source_url: 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page', count:10}
verification_steps: Confirm every saved record contains non-empty url, link_text, and description fields
Confirm the saved source_of_description points back to the snippet_source_url or the article URL
details.reasoning: Saving the final compiled list ensures the deliverable is preserved and can be returned or referenced later; it also provides a clear, verifiable artifact of the work.
